TDB implements this bilateral call, but implementation alone does not prove that TDB Core Funding is its permanent parent.

The official TDB page lists sustainable energy, advanced digital technologies, microelectronics/power electronics and electric vehicles among the call priorities.

actions.scheme_name: 1st Open Call under RDI Fund through TDB as Second-Level Fund Manager

matches.scheme_name: 1st Open Call under RDI Fund through TDB as Second-Level Fund Manager

item.scheme_name: 1st Open Call under RDI Fund through TDB as Second-Level Fund Manager
decision_reasons: The official TDB page explicitly includes DPIIT-defined startups among eligible technology entities receiving RDIF support through SLFMs.
RDIF is the permanent fund
TDB is its designated Second-Level Fund Manager for this application route.
Open status is supported by the active official Apply Now route and operational RDIF registration form
no closing date is published.
The official RDIF/TDB pages enumerate sunrise and strategic sectors including clean energy, quantum, robotics, space, AI, agriculture, health, biotechnology, medical devices and the digital economy.
recommended_admin_actions: Verify beneficiary layer, parent scheme, status evidence, sectors and official links before approval.

validated_record.scheme_name: 1st Open Call under RDI Fund through TDB as Second-Level Fund Manager
target_beneficiaries: Startups, innovators and other directly eligible applicants
objectives: The official TDB page explicitly includes DPIIT-defined startups among eligible technology entities receiving RDIF support through SLFMs.
eligibility: Indian legal entities including DPIIT-defined startups, companies, partnerships and LLPs; resident-Indian control, Indian headquarters and RDI-intensive technology at TRL 4 or above are required
benefits: Long-tenure collateral-free loan, equity or hybrid support; normally up to 50% of assessed project cost. No grant funding. TDB states 3%–4% interest and a 12–15 year period for RDIF loans.

amount_mentions: Long-tenure collateral-free loan, equity or hybrid support; normally up to 50% of assessed project cost. No grant funding. TDB states 3%–4% interest and a 12–15 year period for RDIF loans.

source_evidence.title: Official call page
source_evidence.evidence_text: The official TDB RDIF-SLFM page displays an active Apply Now link to the RDIF registration portal; the TDB page was last updated on 08/07/2026.

source_evidence.evidence_text: The official TDB RDIF-SLFM page displays an active Apply Now link to the RDIF registration portal; the TDB page was last updated on 08/07/2026.

field_evidence.parent_relationship: RDIF is the permanent fund; TDB is its designated Second-Level Fund Manager for this application route.
